title: Ulmus × vegeta
taxonomy: Ulmus × vegeta is a hybrid cultivar of the elm tree, specifically a cross between U. glabra and U. minor. This hybrid cultivar is known for its tolerance to Dutch elm disease and is often used in urban forestry. The classification of U. × vegeta is typically as a cultivar of the Ulmaceae family. In horticultural contexts, it is often designated as a cultivar (U. × vegeta 'Horstmann') or a species (Ulmus × vegeta) depending on the source. It is worth noting that the classification may vary depending on the specific cultivar or source.

intro: Ulmus × vegeta, also known as the hybrid elm, is a sterile hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Ulmus glabra (Wych Elm) and Ulmus minor (Field Elm). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ulmus × vegeta include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and oligomers. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare a range of herbal products, including teas, infusions, and extracts, often combined with other herbs to promote overall well-being and support digestive health.
